cities.name: Srinagar
cities.overview: Srinagar is the summer capital of Jammu & Kashmir, famous for its pristine Dal Lake, Mughal gardens, houseboats, and snow-capped Himalayan backdrop. A perfect blend of natural beauty, rich culture, and timeless Kashmiri hospitality.
cities.history: Founded by Emperor Ashoka, Srinagar flourished under Mughal, Afghan, Sikh, and Dogra rule. The Mughals built the iconic gardens, while the British popularised houseboats on Dal Lake.

places.name: Dal Lake & Houseboat Stay
places.open_hours: All day (Shikara rides: sunrise to sunset)
places.description: The iconic heart of Srinagar — a large urban lake famous for colourful Shikara rides, floating markets, and luxurious houseboats with intricately carved wooden interiors. The lake is surrounded by the Zabarwan mountains and offers breathtaking views at dawn and dusk.
places.tips: Stay overnight on a traditional houseboat with Kashmiri cuisine included. Early morning Shikara ride offers the best views and photography opportunities. Avoid swimming due to water quality; stick to authorised operators.

places.name: Shalimar Bagh
places.description: The most beautiful Mughal garden in Kashmir, built by Emperor Jahangir in 1619, featuring terraced lawns, cascading fountains, Chinar trees, and pavilions overlooking Dal Lake. A UNESCO tentative World Heritage site.
places.tips: Visit in the evening during summer when the fountains are lit. Best during tulip season (March–April) or autumn when the Chinar leaves turn golden. Guided tours explain the Mughal engineering.

places.name: Nishat Bagh
places.description: The largest Mughal garden in Kashmir, known as the 'Garden of Pleasure', with 12 terraces offering stunning views of Dal Lake and the surrounding mountains. Built by Asaf Khan in 1633.
places.tips: Combine with a visit to Shalimar Bagh on the same day. The sunset view from the top terrace is breathtaking. Carry water and wear comfortable shoes for walking the terraces.

places.name: Hazratbal Shrine
places.description: The most revered Muslim shrine in Kashmir, housing the sacred Moi-e-Muqaddas (hair of Prophet Muhammad). The white marble structure on the banks of Dal Lake is an architectural masterpiece.
places.tips: Visit during Friday prayers for the spiritual atmosphere. Women should cover their heads. The lakeside promenade offers beautiful evening views.

places.name: Pahalgam (Day Trip)
places.description: Picturesque valley famous for the Lidder River, Betaab Valley, and as the base for Amarnath Yatra. Lush meadows, pine forests, and snow-capped peaks make it a must-visit day trip from Srinagar.
places.tips: Hire a local taxi for the day. Best for pony rides and riverside picnics. Carry warm layers as temperature drops in the evening.

places.name: Jama Masjid Srinagar
places.description: One of the largest mosques in Kashmir, built in 1400 CE with 370 wooden pillars and a unique Indo-Saracenic style. A centre of Kashmiri Islamic culture.
places.tips: Visit on a Friday for the grand congregational prayer. Remove shoes and dress modestly. The courtyard is peaceful for reflection.

places.name: Indira Gandhi Memorial Tulip Garden
places.description: Asia’s largest tulip garden with over 1.5 million tulips in 60+ varieties spread across terraced slopes overlooking Dal Lake.
places.tips: Visit in late March to mid-April when the tulips are in full bloom. Early morning or late afternoon light is best for photography.

cities.name: Gulmarg
cities.overview: Gulmarg is one of Asia’s premier ski destinations and a breathtaking meadow surrounded by snow-capped peaks. Famous for its Gondola ride — the highest in the world — and as a summer paradise for meadows and wildflowers.
cities.history: Developed by the British as a summer retreat, Gulmarg became India’s first ski destination in the 1920s. The Gulmarg Gondola was inaugurated in 2005.

places.name: Gulmarg Gondola
places.description: Asia’s highest cable car ride taking visitors to Kongdoori (3,747m) and Apharwat Peak (4,000m+) with panoramic Himalayan views. In winter it serves as the main access to ski slopes.
places.tips: Book Phase 1 & 2 tickets in advance during peak season. Dress warmly even in summer. Best for skiing in winter; carry ski gear or rent on-site.

places.name: Gulmarg Meadow
places.description: Vast rolling meadow famous for wildflowers in summer and as a skiing paradise in winter. Offers horse riding, golf in summer, and breathtaking views of the Pir Panjal range.
places.tips: Rent a pony for a leisurely ride across the meadow. Visit in July–August for the flower bloom. Golf course is one of the highest in the world.

places.name: Khilanmarg
places.description: A high-altitude meadow above Gulmarg reached by a short trek or pony ride, offering 360-degree views of the Himalayan range and perfect for summer picnics.
places.tips: The 2-km trek is moderate. Carry water and snacks. Best visited in the morning for clear views.

places.name: St. Mary’s Church
places.description: A charming colonial-era wooden church built in 1895, one of the oldest in Kashmir, set against the backdrop of snow-capped peaks.
places.tips: Peaceful spot for quiet reflection. The interior woodwork is beautifully preserved.

cities.name: Pahalgam
cities.overview: Pahalgam is a picturesque valley known for the Lidder River, lush meadows, pine forests, and as the base for the Amarnath Yatra.
cities.history: Once a favourite summer retreat of Kashmiri royalty, Pahalgam gained popularity with tourists for its scenic beauty and adventure opportunities.

places.name: Lidder River & Betaab Valley
places.description: Crystal-clear Lidder River flowing through Betaab Valley — one of the most photographed locations in Kashmir with snow-capped peaks and lush green meadows.
places.tips: Horse riding and river rafting are popular. Visit early morning for fewer crowds and better light. Carry a picnic basket for the riverside.

places.name: Aru Valley
places.description: A pristine alpine valley surrounded by snow peaks, ideal for short treks, camping, and photography. The starting point for many high-altitude treks.
places.tips: The drive from Pahalgam is scenic. Carry warm layers even in summer. Local shepherds offer guided treks.

places.name: Chandanwari
places.description: The last motorable point on the Amarnath Yatra route, offering dramatic views of the Lidder Valley and the starting point for the holy trek.
places.tips: Pony rides to nearby viewpoints are available. The area is rich in Himalayan flora.

places.name: Lidder River Rafting
places.description: Grade 2–3 white-water rafting on the Lidder River through scenic gorges and pine forests — an exhilarating experience for beginners and experts.
places.tips: Book with licensed operators only. Wear provided safety gear. Best in May–June when water levels are ideal.

cities.name: Jammu
cities.overview: Jammu is the winter capital of Jammu & Kashmir, known as the 'City of Temples' with a rich Dogra cultural heritage and as the gateway to the Kashmir Valley.
cities.history: Ruled by the Dogra dynasty for over a century, Jammu served as the winter capital of the princely state of Jammu and Kashmir.

places.name: Vaishno Devi Temple (nearby)
places.description: One of the holiest Hindu shrines dedicated to Goddess Vaishno Devi, attracting millions of pilgrims every year. The trek to the cave shrine is a spiritual journey through the Trikuta mountains.
places.tips: Book helicopter or pony services in advance. Carry warm clothes for higher altitudes. The Bhawan offers free langar and accommodation for pilgrims.

places.name: Raghunath Temple
places.description: The largest temple complex in northern India, dedicated to Lord Rama, built by Maharaja Gulab Singh in 1835. It features gold-plated interiors and intricate Dogra artwork.
places.tips: Visit during evening aarti for a spiritual experience. The temple has beautiful gold-plated interiors and a peaceful atmosphere.

places.name: Bahu Fort & Garden
places.description: 18th-century fort overlooking the Tawi River with a beautiful terraced garden and temple dedicated to Goddess Kali. Offers panoramic city views.
places.tips: The garden is illuminated in the evening. Combine with a visit to the nearby Bagh-e-Bahu for a relaxed walk.

places.name: Amar Mahal Palace Museum
places.description: Former royal residence built in French Renaissance style, now a museum showcasing Dogra paintings, weapons, and artefacts.
places.tips: The library and art gallery are highlights. The palace offers excellent views of the city.

cities.name: Leh
cities.overview: Leh is the capital of Ladakh — a high-altitude cold desert offering dramatic landscapes, ancient monasteries, and adventure tourism.
cities.history: Once a major stop on the Silk Route, Leh was the capital of the Himalayan kingdom of Ladakh. It came under Indian administration after 1947.

nearest_railhead.name: No direct rail; nearest is Jammu
nearest_railhead.note: Then road via Srinagar or Manali

places.name: Shanti Stupa
places.description: A Japanese-built peace pagoda offering panoramic views of Leh town and the surrounding mountains. Built in 1985 to promote world peace.
places.tips: Climb the 500+ steps or take a taxi. Sunset views are spectacular. Carry water and wear sunscreen due to high altitude.

places.name: Leh Palace
places.description: The 17th-century nine-storey palace of the Ladakhi kings, resembling the Potala Palace in Lhasa. Offers stunning views of the Indus Valley.
places.tips: The views from the roof are breathtaking. Combine with a visit to the nearby Namgyal Tsemo Gompa. Wear warm layers due to altitude.

places.name: Hemis Monastery
places.description: The largest and wealthiest monastery in Ladakh, famous for its annual Hemis Festival with masked dances and vibrant thangkas.
places.tips: Visit during the Hemis Festival (June/July) for the grand celebrations. The museum inside displays ancient artefacts.

places.name: Pangong Lake (Day Trip)
places.description: The world-famous brackish lake at 4,350m, known for its changing colours and the setting of the movie '3 Idiots'.
places.tips: Start early for the long drive. Carry oxygen and warm clothes. The lake changes colour dramatically with sunlight.

cities.name: Sonamarg
cities.overview: Sonamarg is a stunning alpine meadow surrounded by glaciers and snow-capped peaks, famous for trekking and as a stop on the Srinagar-Leh highway.
cities.history: Known as the 'Meadow of Gold' due to the golden wildflowers that bloom in summer. It served as an important halting point on ancient trade routes.

places.name: Thajiwas Glacier
places.description: A majestic glacier accessible by a short trek or pony ride, offering stunning views of ice formations and the surrounding Himalayan peaks.
places.tips: Pony rides are available. Carry warm layers as temperature drops significantly near the glacier. The trek is moderate and highly rewarding.

places.name: Zoji La Pass Viewpoint
places.description: Dramatic high mountain pass at 3,528m on the Srinagar-Leh highway, offering breathtaking views of the Himalayan range.
places.tips: Stop here while travelling to Leh. The road is scenic but narrow. Best visited in clear weather.

cities.name: Kargil
cities.overview: Kargil is the second-largest town in Ladakh, known for its dramatic landscapes, apricot orchards, and as the gateway to Zanskar and Suru Valley.
cities.history: Historically a trading post on the Leh-Srinagar route, Kargil gained international attention during the 1999 Kargil War.

places.name: Mulbekh Monastery & Rock Carving
places.description: Famous 8th-century rock carving of Maitreya Buddha and the ancient Mulbekh Gompa perched on a hill overlooking the valley.
places.tips: Stop here while travelling between Kargil and Leh. The carving is visible from the highway. The monastery offers peaceful views.

places.name: Suru Valley (Day Trip)
places.description: Stunning valley known for its dramatic peaks, including Nun and Kun, and apricot orchards. A photographer’s paradise.
places.tips: The drive is scenic. Best in summer when apricots are ripe. Carry warm layers for higher altitudes.

cities.name: Katra
cities.overview: Katra is the base town for the sacred Vaishno Devi pilgrimage, offering a vibrant mix of spirituality, markets, and Himalayan foothills.
cities.history: Grew around the holy shrine of Vaishno Devi, which has been a major pilgrimage site for centuries.

places.name: Vaishno Devi Shrine
places.description: The holy cave shrine of Goddess Vaishno Devi, one of the most revered pilgrimage sites in India, located at 5,200 ft in the Trikuta mountains.
places.tips: The 12-km trek can be done on foot, pony, or helicopter. Book helicopter in advance. Free langar is provided throughout.
